payload.text: I’ll inspect the frontend structure and existing text/copy code paths first, then consolidate around the smallest shared renderer that matches the app’s current patterns.

{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@openai/codex-sdk": "^0.130.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "gray-matter": "^4.0.3",
    "lucide-react": "^0.468.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "ulid": "^2.3.0",
    "zod": "^3.24.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.2",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.4",
    "electron": "^33.2.1",
    "electron-vite": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

The main surfaces are the create-ticket Markdown draft, the ticket detail Markdown body, and generated Codex console messages. I’m going to add a shared React Markdown renderer that builds React elements directly, uses sanitized link handling, and gives both whole-block and fenced-code copy actions without changing the saved Markdown.

The shared renderer and first test coverage are in place. I’m running the existing test harness and typecheck now; any failures here should be mostly TypeScript/test-shape issues because this project compiles tests through esbuild.

The test harness passes, but strict typecheck caught a few JSX typing issues in the new renderer/test helper. I’m tightening those types now rather than loosening compiler settings.
Tests and typecheck are clean. I’m doing a production build next because the change touches renderer imports and CSS, and that catches Vite/Electron bundling issues that `tsc` can miss.

**Summary**
Implemented a shared sanitized Markdown renderer with source-copy and code-block-copy affordances. It now renders create-ticket draft previews, ticket detail previews, clarification text, and generated Codex completion/final-response console events. Stored Markdown/API payloads are unchanged.

**Files Changed**
- [MarkdownBlock.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/components/MarkdownBlock.tsx:1)
- [App.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/App.tsx:112)
- [ClarificationPanel.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/components/ClarificationPanel.tsx:40)
- [styles.css](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/styles.css:849)
- [markdown-block.test.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/markdown-block.test.tsx:34)
- [run-tests.mjs](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/run-tests.mjs:11)

**Commands Run**
- Inspection: `find`, `grep`, `sed`, `nl`, `git status --short`
- Verification: `npm test`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`

**Tests Run**
- `npm test`: passed, 12/12 tests
- `npm run typecheck`: passed
- `npm run build`: passed

**Remaining Risks**
The renderer is intentionally lightweight because the project had no Markdown parser/sanitizer dependency installed. It covers the requested common syntax and sanitization path, but it is not a full CommonMark implementation for deeper edge cases like complex nested lists.

payload.finalResponse:
**Summary**
Implemented a shared sanitized Markdown renderer with source-copy and code-block-copy affordances. It now renders create-ticket draft previews, ticket detail previews, clarification text, and generated Codex completion/final-response console events. Stored Markdown/API payloads are unchanged.

**Files Changed**
- [MarkdownBlock.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/components/MarkdownBlock.tsx:1)
- [App.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/App.tsx:112)
- [ClarificationPanel.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/components/ClarificationPanel.tsx:40)
- [styles.css](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/styles.css:849)
- [markdown-block.test.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/markdown-block.test.tsx:34)
- [run-tests.mjs](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/run-tests.mjs:11)

**Commands Run**
- Inspection: `find`, `grep`, `sed`, `nl`, `git status --short`
- Verification: `npm test`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`

**Tests Run**
- `npm test`: passed, 12/12 tests
- `npm run typecheck`: passed
- `npm run build`: passed

**Remaining Risks**
The renderer is intentionally lightweight because the project had no Markdown parser/sanitizer dependency installed. It covers the requested common syntax and sanitization path, but it is not a full CommonMark implementation for deeper edge cases like complex nested lists.
